text:
I am trying to come back on this platform but this time with a different motive, I want things to be rawer and more authentic here.

Work will always keep on going, it will never end.

There will always be another mail, another meeting, another priority task, another deadline to meet.

But somewhere along the way, it feels like we’ve started forgetting the human side of it all.

We have become so used to functioning, delivering, performing....that we sometimes forget that people are carrying entire emotional worlds behind those LinkedIn profiles and Teams calls.

I’ve realized this deeply after losing my dad.

Most days, things feel normal. I’m working, talking to people, doing everything I’m supposed to do.

And then sometimes, out of nowhere, a small memory appears.
Something he used to say.
Something he would have found funny.
A random moment.
And for a few seconds, the day just feels different.

Grief doesn’t always look dramatic.
It’s often quiet and unexpected.

One moment you’re completely fine, and the next moment your mind drifts somewhere else before you bring yourself back to the present.
And the strange thing is - the world doesn’t pause in those moments.

Work continues.
Notifications keep coming.
People keep moving.
That’s how life works.

Healing was never meant to be efficient.
We are building a world that is becoming more automated, more optimized, more mechanical.
But humans were never designed to process emotions like machines.

Grief takes time.
Grief isn’t a straight line.
It’s waves.

I’m writing this because the world is becoming more mechanical.
We measure productivity, output, performance.
But behind every employee, every colleague, every 'professional' LinkedIn profile - there is still a human being carrying something invisible.
And sometimes all they need is a little patience.

Because coming back to normal after losing someone you love doesn’t happen on a timeline.
You just slowly learn how to carry the grief… while continuing to live your life.
Work never really stops.
But healing takes its own time.

And sometimes it takes the same moment again and again.
So if someone around you seems a little quieter today…
A little distant…
A little slower than usual…
Be kind.
You never know what memory just walked back into their mind.

Work will always be there.
But humanity at work - that’s something we have to consciously protect.

Another uncomfortable thought:
If work is your entire personality, then you’re not living - you’re being utilised.

Some people don’t experience time.

They consume it in slots.
Even rest is 'well-deserved'.
Even joy needs justification.

They don’t sit with thoughts.
They schedule them.

The tragedy isn’t working hard.
The tragedy is outsourcing meaning to a payslip.

Philosophers warned us about this long ago -
when identity collapses into function, humans become tools that applaud their own usefulness.

Titles feel permanent until they aren’t.
Calendars feel important until they stop filling themselves.

And when work finally goes quiet - layoffs, burnout, weekends, retirement -
there’s a strange silence no KPI prepares you for.

Because if you only know how to be productive,
you don’t know how to be present.

Work should sharpen you.
Not replace you.

A life that cannot slow down
is not ambitious -
it’s afraid of stillness.

I sometimes hate how we only post 'happy' moments here and I have always wanted to change it.

So, I am going to start with myself.

No, I didn't get a new job, cracked a new company, got 7 figure salaries etc.

I just want to normalize being 'not delighted/excited' all the time.

We live in a world where everyone is 'grateful', 'honoured', and 'thrilled'.
But rarely do you see - 'tired', 'lost', or 'unsure'.

We all are humans, and we all feel things - not all days are good.

You know what’s truly rare on LinkedIn?
Someone saying: 'I don’t have it figured out, but I’m still trying.'

So, here I am -

I am not doing great, have been fighting - 1 day at a time. I think I will get through it :)

There's nothing more motivational than being myself for me :)

An honest LinkedIn update would look like this:

'Bought my first house today.
Thanks to the brands who paid me…
…to sell you the dream of buying a course that changed my life.'

Reality:
Courses changed something -
Their EMI status.

I don't know why people hate Gen Z so much.

We are so honest.

Aap likhte hoge ki chutti chahiye - beemar hu and all.

We speak the truth.

Chutti chahiye.

Manager asks 'Kyu?'

We say -

Break up ho gaya.
Vibe nahi ho paa raha hai.
I am tired.
Mental break chahiye.
Shopping jaana hai.
pichle week zyada kaam kar liya tha.

The people who are the most jealous of this - Boomers.

They're like - Haww, aisa kaisa?

'Jabb mere chacha ji ki death Hui thi, uss dinn bhi maina kaam kiya tha'

'Mere shaadi Wale dinn, subha bhi I worked - raat ko hi thi na shaadi'

Bruh.
This is not a thing to show off.

Take breaks please, nothing increases productivity more than this.

Sometimes just wonder and sit - Main kaam karr kyu raha hu? Taaki I can enjoy life.

- I can attend my best friend's wedding.
- I can roam the world.
- I can afford things for the people I love.

Pleaseeeee don't make WORK your entire personality.

At least I don't need to revive my dada, dadi, chacha, chachi etc for each job - taaki I can get a leave.

Jitni toxic company, utne ache dost bannte hai uss company mein.

change my mind.

Company chorne ka dukh nahi hai, ache dost milne ki khushi hai :)

We live in a world where…
'You need a medical certificate'
'You need proof for stress'
'You need a reason to not be a machine'

And then there’s this email 👇
'Sir, I recently had a breakup… I need a short break.'

No fancy excuse.
No fake illness.
Just honesty.

Pure human honesty.
We keep talking about mental health.

But we still hesitate to say 'I’m not okay.'
Truth is - heartbreak hurts more than fever.

Anxiety drains more energy than a 12-hour shift.
And emotional trauma doesn’t show up in a blood test.

Maybe it’s time we normalize:
• Taking leave for a bad day
• Crying without shame
• Healing without guilt
• Being human without justification

Work will always be there.
People? Feelings? Peace?

Once lost… they take years to recover.

If someone finds courage to be this honest -
the least we can offer is empathy.

Not every hero wears a cape.
Some just write emails that say,
'I need a break. My heart is broken.'

Let’s build workplaces where vulnerability isn’t a weakness -
it’s respected as a sign of strength.

Yesterday, I got a message:

'Yaar I am stuck in low-paying service-based jobs
… I want to switch, but have no idea how.'

And honestly… this isn’t one person.
This is a mass problem in India.

People earning low salary:
– Working 10–12 hours a day
– Barely any growth
– Random skill learning
– Slowly losing confidence

Everyone knows: 'I need to do better.'
But no one tells them: 'What exactly should I do?'
And that’s the biggest gap.

Not a skill gap.
Not a talent gap.
A clarity gap.

Everywhere you look, it’s the same advice:
– Learn DSA
– Learn SQL
– Build projects

But where’s the roadmap?
The sequence?
The execution system?
Nowhere.

So people spend 6 months watching YouTube…
And end up exactly where they started.

That’s why we decided:
We’re not building another 'course.'
We’re building something else.
A system.
A roadmap.
A way to actually move.

If you:
– Feel stuck in a service-based job
– Want to switch
– But don’t know where to start
This is for you.

No fluff.
No fake motivation.

Just real clarity.

Because the truth is:
You’re not underpaid.
You’re under-guided.

And once that changes…
Everything changes.

Stay tuned!
https://lnkd.in/dqKrdi2b

Most people spend months preparing for a Meta interview, This hackathon skips that entire process...💯

Meta has announced it's biggest National level Hackathon i.e. PyTorch OpenEnv Hackathon in India. And you can get a chance to directly interview at Meta or Hugging Face AI Teams.❤️

Timeline -
Round 1 starts tomorrow, March 25th.
Registration closes April 3rd.
Grand finale is a 48-hour sprint in Bangalore on April 25th and 26th.

Prizes worth 30,000$ and an opportunity to learn.

You'll building on Meta's OpenEnv, the same reinforcement learning environment framework used by leading AI labs. Your code gets reviewed by Meta engineers working on agentic AI right now, not mentors, not alumni, actual engineers shaping the product.

And if you've never touched RL before, they've included free prep courses with Hugging Face and PyTorch plus Meta-led deep dive sessions before every round.

Eligbility - Tech Working Professionals, Developers, College Students
Apply here - https://bit.ly/4sXoFpN
Don't sleep on such opportunities, I have already applied. ❤️

Designer: 'That's not what I made.'
Engineer: 'That's exactly what you gave me.'
Both staring at the same screen. Both convinced they're right...🤡

The design looks perfect in Figma until you push to production and find something is off. You spend 45 minutes comparing screenshots pixel by pixel trying to figure out what changed.

Floto's Design Diff just made that entire process take 45 seconds.
You upload your Figma file and a screenshot of the live implementation. The AI compares them and tells you exactly what's different. Spacing issues, colour mismatches, font inconsistencies, responsive breakpoints. All flagged before your users see them.

It exports discrepancies directly as Linear issues so your team doesn't need a separate handoff conversation.

I've spent 4 years shipping features at Microsoft for 130M users. The gap between what design intended and what engineering shipped is one of the most consistent sources of rework I've seen across every team. Tools that close that gap quietly are the ones that actually stick.

1000 free credits to start, no card required.

hashtag#ai hashtag#developers hashtag#tools hashtag#engineering

PPO at Microsoft! 🎉

I’m thrilled to share that I’ve received a Pre-Placement Offer (PPO) to join Microsoft as a Software Engineer after my internship! 💙

My internship with the Copilot M365 team was nothing short of a rollercoaster — fun, challenging, and full of learning. Over the course of my journey, I got the opportunity to work on 3 impactful projects, constantly diving into design reviews, PR discussions, and countless brainstorming sessions.❤️

It wasn’t just about coding — it was about design thinking, problem-solving, collaboration, and pushing my limits. These experiences truly brought out my potential and helped me clear my exit interviews with confidence and happiness.❤️

From the amazing food & beautiful campus to the goodies and thoughtful gifts, Microsoft made every moment special. But above all, it’s the people — my mentors, teammates, and friends — who made this journey unforgettable. 💙

I’m beyond excited to continue my journey with this beautiful Microsoft family, and I can’t wait to contribute even more in the future! 🚀

Celebrating Our Journey as National Finalists at Myntra WeForShe 2025!

I’m excited to share that Team Synhergy made it to the Grand Finale of the Myntra WeForShe hackathon! Presenting at Myntra HQ, Bengaluru was an incredible experience.

From ideation to engineering to presenting our solution, the journey was deeply enriching. Out of 18,000+ teams, our team was selected as one of the Top 6 Finalists, making this achievement even more meaningful for all of us.

During the ideation phase, we explored three problem themes: Hyper-Personalisation, The Gen Z Way of Shopping, and Intelligent Fashion Shopping Experiences. Combining these themes, we aimed to build a solution that was both technically challenging and highly relevant to modern shoppers.
Our solution, VibeVault, is an AI-powered fashion shopping assistant designed to reimagine how users discover, compare, and engage with fashion online. It addresses key challenges such as wishlist abandonment, review overload, and lack of contextual personalisation by leveraging Generative AI and social intelligence to create a seamless and interactive experience. The prototype was developed using the MERN Stack and Hugging Face Models. The system incorporated:
 • Social-circle-based recommendations using BERT and CLIP
 • AI-powered product review summarisation using BART
 • Intelligent wishlist comparison using SBERT
We demonstrated a fully functional MVP to 30+ users, validating its scalability, usability, and ability to deliver a personalised, data-driven shopping experience.

A heartfelt thanks to the Myntra team for creating such an inspiring platform that truly empowers women in technology. Deeply inspired by Nandita Sinha's words on innovation and authenticity. I’m also grateful to our mentor Nishanth Aithal for his invaluable guidance, and to the Myntra HackerRamp organizing team — Neha Roy, Sumit Kumar, and Vidhyashree K — for curating such a well-structured and impactful program.

This experience has been all about learning, innovation, and growth✨

hashtag#Myntra hashtag#WeForShe hashtag#HackerRamp hashtag#WomenInTech hashtag#FashionTech hashtag#AI hashtag#GenerativeAI hashtag#HyperPersonalisation hashtag#TechInnovation

Sharing an Incredible Summer at Microsoft ✨

Posting this a little late, but some moments deserve to be celebrated no matter when you talk about them. It’s been a little while since I completed my Software Engineering Internship at Microsoft, and I’m still grateful for how enriching and transformative the experience was.

During my internship, I had the wonderful opportunity to work with the Azure CXP (Customer Experience Platform) team. I contributed to building intelligent and reliable solutions by leveraging Microsoft’s Semantic Kernel for integrating large language models and creating seamless integrations with the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. Being part of such a talented and supportive group taught me more than I could have imagined—about building reliable systems, solving meaningful problems, and growing as an engineer.

I’m deeply thankful to my manager, Venkata Durga Prasad Popuri, whose guidance made a huge difference throughout this journey. I’m equally grateful to my mentor, Ravi Gupta, for constantly supporting me and pushing me to learn and improve. A special thanks to Sachin Trivedi and Mukesh Chandra Gupta for always being there to help and encourage me.

And with all this, I’m truly excited to share that I’ve received a Pre-Placement Offer (PPO) from Microsoft. This opportunity means a lot, and I’m looking forward to the next chapter with immense gratitude and excitement.

Excited for the next chapter and the opportunities ahead! ✨ 💚

hashtag#Microsoft hashtag#PPO hashtag#Internship hashtag#Azure hashtag#AI hashtag#SoftwareEngineering hashtag#TechInterns hashtag#CloudComputing hashtag#WomenInTech hashtag#CareerJourney hashtag#Grateful
